title: MD
biography: Archibald Cochrane was a Scottish physician and epidemiologist who championed the use of randomized controlled trials and evidence-based medicine. His 1972 book 'Effectiveness and Efficiency' argued that healthcare should be guided by rigorous evidence. The Cochrane Collaboration, the world's leading evidence synthesis organization, is named in his honor.
aiSummary: Archibald Cochrane was an epidemiology specialist who pioneered evidence-based medicine. The Cochrane Collaboration is named in his honor. Based in Cardiff, United Kingdom.
techniquesInvented: Advocacy for randomized controlled trials as the gold standard in medicine

citations.title: Effectiveness and Efficiency: Random Reflections on Health Services

timeline.title: Director of MRC Epidemiology Unit in Cardiff
timeline.description: Led pioneering studies on evidence-based healthcare delivery

timeline.title: Published 'Effectiveness and Efficiency'
timeline.description: Landmark book arguing that all medical treatments should be evaluated by randomized controlled trials

timeline.title: Cochrane Collaboration founded
timeline.description: Global organization for systematic reviews named in his honor, founded five years after his death
